steps.title: Find the minimum of f(x) using the vertex formula
steps.content:
For $f(x) = 3x^2 + 54x + 247$, identify $a = 3$ and $b = 54$.

Substitute into the vertex formula:
$$x = -\frac{54}{2(3)} = -\frac{54}{6} = -9$$
The minimum of $f(x)$ occurs at $x = -9$.

steps.title: Apply the transformation g(x) = f(x+4)
steps.content:
Since $g(x) = f(x+4)$, the function $g$ reaches its minimum when its input equals the x-value where $f$ is minimized.

Set the argument equal to $-9$:
$$x + 4 = -9$$
Solve for $x$:
$$x = -13$$
That confirms A is correct.
